def: s.f. (abst. s. fr. the preceding), The state of being unfettered, or freed, &c.; freedom; emancipation; the being loosed (from an obligation, &c.).
s.m. (abst. s. fr. the preceding), The state of being unfettered, or freed, &c.; freedom; emancipation; the being loosed (from an obligation, &c.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Unfettered, &c. (i.q. nirbandh); — one who is liberated, or freed, &c.; a freedman; one who is loosed (from an obligation).

adj. (f. -inī), Insisting (upon); persistent, pertinacious; persevering; intent (on); determined, resolute; obstinate, dogged; importunate; pressing, urgent; — persistently devoted (to spiritual things, &c.).
adj. Without relations, or kindred, friendless.
adj. (f. -ā), Without family or lineage; without issue, childless; extinct (race or family).
more: adj. 1. Childless, without offspring. 2. Extinct (as a race or family).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without family or lineage; without issue, childless; extinct (race or family).
adj. Without power, or authority, or influence, &c.; powerless; helpless; enfeebled.
adj. = نربش nir-bas, q.v. adj. Without power, or authority, or influence, &c.; powerless; helpless; enfeebled.

adj. & adv. Excessive, exceeding, vehement, violent; ardent; deep, sound (as sleep); much; (in comp.) full (of), filled (with); — excessively, exceedingly, very much, &c.; — s.m. Intent and pertinacious pursuit or purpose; determined bent (of the mind, &c.); fulness of delight (in); complete conversancy (with); — resting (upon); anything to rest or depend upon; a prop, a fulcrum; — reliance, dependence, trust.
adj. (f. -ā), Fearless, undaunted; bold, daring; not afraid (of, -se), unapprehensive (of); — free from likelihood of danger or harm, secure.
more: adj. Fearless, free from fear, undaunted.

def: s.f. A fearless woman.
s.f. Fearlessness; freedom from apprehension, or from danger; security.
adj. Without food, destitute of food, foodless.

adj. (f. -ā), Without seed; seedless (as certain kinds of grapes, &c.); impotent; without issue, childless; extinct (as a race or family); extirpated, annihilated; — without foundation, groundless (as a statement, or report).
more: adj. 1. Without seed, childless. 2. Extinct (a race or family).

def: adj. & adv. = nir-vikalp, q.v. adj. Not admitting an alternative; recognizing no such distinction as that of subject and object; — not admitting of difference or otherness (between one's own spirit and the divine essence); — of unchanging purpose; being 'without variableness or shadow of turning' (the Deity; — syn. bhed-rahit).
adj. = nir-vikār, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Unchanged, unaltered; unchangeable, immutable, uniform.
s.f. Zedoary, Cucurma (or Amomum) zedoaria (a spicy plant, somewhat like ginger in its leaves, but of a sweet scent); — a species of grass, Kyllingia monocephala (used as an antidote).
more: n. f. S. निर्विषी Zedoary; curcumazedoaria , Roxb.
s. f. Zedoary (a spicy plant somewhat like ginger in its leaves, but of a sweet scent. Amomum zedoaria: Willd. Curcuma zedoaria: Roxb. ).

def: adj. Without knowledge, uninstructed, unlettered, ignorant; — without understanding, senseless; incapable of receiving instruction; — knowing without having been instructed or taught, possessed of intuitive knowledge.
adj. Without understanding, senseless.
adj. Destitute of reason or understanding, senseless, irrational, witless, ignorant, stupid; out of one's wits.
more: adj. Void of understanding, senseless, irrational.

def: s.f. Senselessness, folly.
adj. Not to be understood, incomprehensible.
s.m. A kind of pot-herb, Corchorus capsularis.
more: n. m. A potherb; Corchorus capsularis , Wat.
s. m. A kind of greens. (Corchorus capsularis).

def: adj. Guileless, simple.
s.m. The plant Semecarpus anacardium; — the marking-nut.
s.f. A species of creeper, Sanseviera roxburghiana, or S. zeylanica (from the fibres of which bow-strings are made).
s.m. = nirdayatā, or nirdayā, q.v. s.f. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.m. A cave, a cavern (i.q. dara).
adj. = نردئي nir-daʼī, or nir-dayī, q.v.
s.f. = S نرديتا निर्दयता nir-dayatā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.f. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.m. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتا निर्दयता nir-dayatā, s.f. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
adj. Pitiless, merciless, hard-hearted, unkind, unfeeling, severe, cruel.

s.m. Pointing out, directing, ordering; direction, order, command; instruction; declaration; description, designation; depicting; detailed account, specification, special mention; — certainty, ascertainment.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without property or wealth; moneyless; poor, needy, indigent; — a poor man, a pauper
subs: v.t. To strip of property or wealth, to reduce to poverty, to beggar.

def: s.f. = S نردهنتو निर्धनत्व nir-dhanatva, s.m. Absence of property or wealth; poverty, indigence, pauperism.
s.m. = S نردهنتا निर्धनता nir-dhanatā, s.f. Absence of property or wealth; poverty, indigence, pauperism.

s.m. Specification (of one out of many), particularizing; distinguishing the merits (of); — fixing or determining with accuracy; looking or inquiring (into); — determinedness, ascertainment (of a matter or subject); accuracy; certainty, positive assurance (of), persuasion; — a determination, resolution, positive purpose
more: s. m. Settling or fixing with accuracy, ascertainment.
subs: (-kā), To specify; to fix with accuracy, to distinguish (between), &c.

def: s.m. Ascertaining, determining, settling, fixing; ascertainment, &c. (i.q. nir-dhār); — agreement, arrangement.
part. (f. -ā), Defined, particularized; determined, ascertained; fixed, settled.
s.m. Injustice; unrighteousness; irreligion, impiety; — adj. (f. -ā), or -ī), Destitute of law or religion; unjust, unrighteous; immoral; irreligious; impious.
part. (f. -ā), Pointed out, shown; described, depicted; ordered, directed; specified, particularized; assigned; ascertained, determined.
adj. Blameless.
adj. corr. of nir-dosh, q.v. adj. Faultless; without defect or blemish, unblemished; blameless, guiltless, sinless, innocent
subs: or nir-dosh rakhnā, v.t. To prove to be guiltless or innocent, to clear from guilt, to exculpate, exonerate, absolve, acquit.

def: adj. Faultless; without defect or blemish, unblemished; blameless, guiltless, sinless, innocent
subs: or nir-dosh rakhnā, v.t. To prove to be guiltless or innocent, to clear from guilt, to exculpate, exonerate, absolve, acquit.

def: s.f. = S نردوشتو निर्दोषत्व nīr-doshatva, s.m. Faultlessness, blamelessness, innocence, &c.
s.m. = S نردوشتا निर्दोषता nir-doshatā, s.f. Faultlessness, blamelessness, innocence, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Faultless, &c. (i.q. nir-dosh, q.v.); — a blameless or guiltless man, innocent one.

adj. Free from either of two alternatives, indifferent in regard to opposite pairs of feelings (as pleasure and pain), neither glad nor sorry, &c.; not standing in mutual relation (to), not dependent (upon another), independent; — not striving together; free from envy or jealousy; — not causing disputes, uncontested, undisputed; — not double; not acknowledging two principles; — adv. Without quarrel or dispute, peaceably, &c.
adj. (in Gram.) Without reph, q.v.
s.m. Going out, going forth, setting out, going away, departure; receding, vanishiug; — (nir-gam) exit, issue, outlet; a door.
s.m. Going out, going forth, setting out, going away, departure; receding, vanishiug; — (nir-gam) exit, issue, outlet; a door.
adj. (f. -ā), Void of smell or scent, scentless, inodorous, unfragrant
subs: s.f. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum.

def: s.f. Absence of smell, want of fragrance, scentlessness.
part. (f. -ā), Gone out, gone forth, issued; come forth, come out, appeared; gone away, departed; disappeared, vanished; extinct.
s.m. A collection or list of words; a vocabulary; a table of contents, an index.
s.m. A vocabulary, &c. (i.q. nir-ghaṇṭ); — singling out the words of a sentence and writing them down in their independent form.
adj. Not odious, not detestable; — pitiless, merciless, cruel; — without shame, shameless; — detestable, abominable.
more: adj. 1. Not odious, not detestable. 2. Abominable.

def: s.f. Pitilessness, unmercifuluess, hardheartedness, cruelty.

adj. (f. -ā), Devoid of all qualities or properties; without attributes; — devoid of good qualities or virtues; unskilful; worthless, bad, vicious; — without a string, stringless, unstrung (as a necklace, &c.); — s.m. The Supreme Being (as without qualities of any kind).
more: adj. 1. Without passions or human qualities (an epithet of God). 2. Without estimable qualities, worthless, unskilful. 3. Unstrung (as, a necklace).

def: s.f. = S نرگنيو निर्गुणत्व nir-guṇatva, s.m. Absence of qualities, want of properties; freedom from all qualities (as an attribute of the Supreme Being); — absence or want of good qualities, worthlessness, wickedness, viciousness, baseness.

s.m. = S نرگنتا निर्गुणता nir-guṇatā, s.f. Absence of qualities, want of properties; freedom from all qualities (as an attribute of the Supreme Being); — absence or want of good qualities, worthlessness, wickedness, viciousness, baseness.
s.m. One who believes the Supreme Being to be void of all qualities; a believer in one Supreme Spirit without qualities of any kind.

ety: for nir-guṇṭī = S. निर्गुण्टी or निर्गुण्टिका
def: s.f. The shrub Vitex negundo; — the root of a lotus.
adj. Without laughter, or averse to laughter; grave; dull.
s.f. = nir-īkshā, q.v. s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
s.f. = nir-īkshā, q.v. s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
s.f. = S نريہتو निरीहत्व nir-īhatva, s.m. Indifference; absence of desire or effect.
s.m. = S نريہتا निरीहता nir-īhatā, s.f. Indifference; absence of desire or effect.

ety: nirīkh˚ or nirekh˚ = Prk. निरिक्ख(इ) = S. निरीक्ष(ते), rt. ईक्ष् with निस्
def: v.t. & n. To look at or towards, to behold, gaze at, regard, observe, view; — to look out, to spy; — to consider, inspect; — to expect, hope for (i.q. nirakhnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. To behold, to spy, to look out.

def: s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
s.m. A look; looking at, regarding, seeing; — expecting, hope; — aspect (of the planets).
adj. = nir-īksha-mān, q.v. adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
part. (f. -ā), Looked at, beheld; considered; — expected, hoped.
adj. (f. -ā), Not having the use of the limbs; mutilated, maimed.
adj. Without owner or proprietor; without a master, or ruler.
s.m. The body of a plough (without the pole and ploughshare).
s.m. The body of a plough (without the pole and ploughshare).
adj. (f. -ā), Without water, waterless, dry, desert; — not mixed with water (as milk, &c.); — s.m. A desert, waste; — a fast in which water even is not to be drunk
subs: (˚la+ek), s.f. Name of the eleventh day in the light half of the month Jeṭh (May-June), on which even the drinking of water is forbidden; the fast of that day
To go without food and water, to keep a rigid fast.

def: s.m. A religious penance or fast in which water is not drunk the whole day (see nirjalaikādaśī, s.v. nirjal).
adj. Unpeopled, uninhabited, unfrequented; depopulated, deserted, lonely, desolate.
more: adj. Without a person or individual, desert, uninhabited.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Not becoming old; undecaying, imperishable, immortal; — s.m. (S. nir-jaram) Ambrosia, the food of the gods.
s.m. Conquest, complete victory, vanquishing, subduing.
s.m. (f. -trī), Victor, conqueror, vanquisher.
s.m. (f. -trī), Victor, conqueror, vanquisher.
s.m. A waterfall, cascade, cataract, a mountain-torrent; a spring.
part. (f. -ā), Conquered, completely vanquished, thoroughly subdued, overcome; won, gained; — unconquered.
adj. (f. -ā), Without life, lifeless, inanimate, dead; — s.m. Lifelessness, death.
= H نرجوش निर्जोष nir-josh (dialec. forms, occurring in the Rāmāyaṇ) = niśćay.
= H نرجوس निर्जोस nir-jos (dialec. forms, occurring in the Rāmāyaṇ) = niśćay.
adv. corr. of nish-kewal, q.v. adj. & adv. Belonging exclusively (to); — exclusively, solely; entirely, completely; merely, only.

ety: prob. akin to S. निरीक्ष्, and H. nirakhnā
def: s.m. Tariff, assize, price (of provisions, &c., as fixed by the magistrates or the police), market-rate, current price
more: n. m. Market rate ( bāzār kā nirkh ); current price. nirkh-bandī . Adjustment or statement of rates. nirkh dārogah , An officer who regulates the market rates. nirkh muqqarrar k .; H bhāo nikālnā , v. a. To fix the selling price. nirkh-nāmah , A table of price currents. nirkh-nāmah hunḍyāṅ . Course of exchange.
s. m. Price (fixed by the officers of police), price current, tariff, assize.
subs: s.f. Adjustment of rates and prices; — a statement or table of prices, price-list, rate
s.m. An officer who regulates the market-rates, clerk of the market
s.m. A table of current prices, a price-current
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نرخ (nirx). Compare Bengali নিরিখ (nirikh), Malayalam നിരക്ക് (nirakkŭ).

def: s.m. One who looks at, or views, &c. (syn. dekhne-hārā), a viewer, a beholder; an inspector, examiner (cf. P. nirḵẖī).

ety: nirḵẖ, q.v.+ī = S. इन्; — cf. S. nir-īkshin
def: s.m. The person who fixes the price-current (i.q. nirḵẖ-darog̠a); an appraiser, assigner, assizer.
more: s. m. The person who fixes the price current, an appraiser, assizer.

def: s. m. An account of the price current.
adj. (f. -ā), Shameless, immodest, impudent, brazen-faced; — s.m. A shameless person.
s.f. A shameless woman; — shamelessness, immodesty; impudence; — obscenity.
s.f. = S نرلجتو निर्लज्जत्व nir-lajjatva, s.m. Shamelessness, &c. (i.q. nir-lajjā, q.v.).
s.m. = S نرلجتا निर्लज्जता nir-lajjatā, s.f. Shamelessness, &c. (i.q. nir-lajjā, q.v.).
adj. Free from spot, spotless, stainless, unsullied (cf. nir-lipt).
part. (f. -ā), Unconnected (with); — unsmeared, unanointed; unstained, undefiled, uncontaminated; — s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from desire; without avarice or greed, unavaricious, uncovetous; contented; — s.m. Absence of desire, or avarice, or covetousness; unselfishness; contentment.
adj. (f. -inī), Free from avarice, &c. (i.q. nir-lobh); — s.m. One who is free from desire, or covetousness, &c.
more: adj. Not covetous, void of avarice, contented.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Unintoxicated, sober; quiet; — free from arrogance or haughtiness, not proud, humble; — not in rut (as an elephant); — s.m. An elephant out of rut, an elephant after the frontal juice has ceased to exude.

ety: ˚maʼe = Ap. Prk. मएवं or मइव्वं = Prk. मएअव्वं = S. मातव्यं, rt. मा
def: inf. n. or gerund (dialec.; Rām.), Making, forming, &c. (i.q. nir-māṇ, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَرْمِی (narmī). By surface analysis, نَرْم (narm) + ـی (-ī).

ety: nir+Prk. मल्लं = S. माल्यं; — in Prk. निम्मल्लं
def: adj. & s.m. = nir-mālya, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Clean, pure, clear, &c. (i.q. nir-mal, q.v.); — s.m. Cleanness, purity, clearness, stainlessness; — the remains of an offering (to a deity); flowers left at a sacrificial ceremony.
adj. (f. -ā), = H نرملا निरमला nir-malā [S. निर्मल+कः], adj. (f. -ī), Free from dirt or impurities, spotless, stainless, unsullied; clear, clean, pure, limpid; transparent; — cloudless; bright.
more: adj. Pure, limpid, clear, pellucid, transparent, spotless, clean.

def: adj. (f. -ī), = S نرمل निर्मल and H. निरमल nir-mal, adj. (f. -ā), Free from dirt or impurities, spotless, stainless, unsullied; clear, clean, pure, limpid; transparent; — cloudless; bright.
more: adj. Pure, limpid, clear, pellucid, transparent, spotless, clean.

def: s.f. = S نرملتو निर्मलत्व nir-malatva, s.m. Cleanness, purity (physical or moral), spotlessness, stainlessness; clearness; limpidness; transparency; cloudlessness.
more: s. f. Pureness, limpidness, pellucidness, cleanness, clearness, transparency.

def: s.m. = S نرملتا निर्मलता nir-malatā, s.f. Cleanness, purity (physical or moral), spotlessness, stainlessness; clearness; limpidness; transparency; cloudlessness.
s.f. The seed of Strychnos potatorum (with which water is cleared); clearing-nut.
more: s. f. Name of a seed with which water is cleared. (Strychnos potatorum).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Clean, pure, clear, &c. (i.q. nir-mal, q.v.); — s.m. Cleanness, purity, clearness, stainlessness; — the remains of an offering (to a deity); flowers left at a sacrificial ceremony.
adj. (f. -ā), Devoid of egoism; unselfish; disinterested; claiming, or wishing for, nothing; regardless (of), indifferent (to); free from all connection with the outer world.
s.f. Unselfishness; disinterestedness; disregard of worldly possessions or interests; indifference (towards).
s.m. = nir-mamatā, q.v. s.f. Unselfishness; disinterestedness; disregard of worldly possessions or interests; indifference (towards).

s.m. Measuring or meting out; measure; reach, extent; — forming, making, producing, creating; contriving; constructing; formation, fabrication, manufacture, production, creation; composition; work; a building; — a part; — the best part (of anything), pith, marrow, cream, essence; — propriety, fitness ( = S. nir-māṇā)
more: s. m. 1. Manufacture, making, production. 2. Propriety, fitness.

ety: nirmā˚ = S. निर्मा(ति), rt. मा with निस्
def: v.t. To make, form, fashion, fabricate; to produce, create; to build, construct; to compose.
adj. Without a man; deserted by men, unpeopled, uninhabited.
adj. Without a man; deserted by men, unpeopled, uninhabited.
s.m. (f. -trī), Maker, builder, creator, former, producer.

ety: ˚mayaʼu = Ap. Prk. मएव्वउं = Prk. मएअव्वअं = S. मातव्य+कं, rt. mā
def: part. adj. (dialec.; Rām.), Formed, fashioned, made, &c. (i.q. nir-mit, q.v.).
part. (f. -ā), Constructed, manufactured, produced, formed, fashioned, fabricated, made; artificial; — meted out; decreed.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from illusion, or fascination; not fascinated; free from attachment; without affection; unkind; — one who is wanting in affection, &c.
more: (from निर् prep. and मोह Fainting, &c.) adj. Not fascinated, without affection, free from attachment, unkind.

def: s.m. Setting loose or free, liberating; — casting off, shedding; — a hide, or skin; the slough (of a snake); — name of a son of the eighth Manu; name of one of the Saptarshis under the thirteenth Manu.
adj. (f. -ā), Without root, rootless; eradicated; — without origin or foundation, baseless, unfounded.
To water; — to provide with food.
n. m. S. निर्णय 1. ( Logic ) Inference; conclusion. 2. ( Law ) Adjudication; award. nirne patr . The award; verdict. nirne k. , v. a. 1. To distinguish discriminate. 2. To adjudge; decide.

s.f. corr. of nir-ṇay, q.v. s.m. Removing, removal; — investigation; discussion; complete ascertainment, decision, certainty, determination, precise conclusion, proof; precise definition, settlement; (in Logic) deduction, inference, conclusion, demonstration; application of a conclusive argument; — (in Law) decision, sentence, decree, verdict, award
subs: (-kā), To ascertain, determine; to distinguish, discriminate (between); to adjudge, decide, &c. (see nirṇay).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without guardian, or master, or superior; protectorless, unprotected; — s.m. One who is protectorless, &c.; an orphan.
s.f. An unprotected woman; — a widow.
s.f. = S نرناتهتو निर्नाथत्व nir-nāthatva, s.m. The being without a master, or guardian, or protector; — want of protection; — widowhood; orphanage.
s.m. = S نرناتهتا निर्नाथता nir-nāthatā, s.f. The being without a master, or guardian, or protector; — want of protection; — widowhood; orphanage.

r: nir-ṇay, nir-ṇai, nir-ṇaʼe, nirnay, nir-nai, nir-naʼe, nir-ne, nī, nis
def: s.m. Removing, removal; — investigation; discussion; complete ascertainment, decision, certainty, determination, precise conclusion, proof; precise definition, settlement; (in Logic) deduction, inference, conclusion, demonstration; application of a conclusive argument; — (in Law) decision, sentence, decree, verdict, award
more: adj. Fixed, established, certain, appointed. s. m. Ascertainment.
subs: (-kā), To ascertain, determine; to distinguish, discriminate (between); to adjudge, decide, &c. (see nirṇay).

def: deciding, determining, conclusive; crucial, decisive
s.m. (f. -trī), One who verifies, or demonstrates, &c.; one who pronounces a sentence, a judge.
s.m. (f. -trī), One who verifies, or demonstrates, &c.; one who pronounces a sentence, a judge.
part. (f. ā), Deduced, traced out, brought to a conclusion, ascertained, determined, settled, decided, proved; adjudged, decreed, sentenced.

s.m. Locking up, confinement, imprisonment; — enclosing, covering up; obstruction, hindrance, prevention; — check, restraint, coercion, suppression; — annihilation, destruction; — disappointment, frustration of hope.

s.m. Confining; confinement, &c. (i.q. nirodh, q.v.). s.m. Locking up, confinement, imprisonment; — enclosing, covering up; obstruction, hindrance, prevention; — check, restraint, coercion, suppression; — annihilation, destruction; — disappointment, frustration of hope.

adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
more: adj. In high health and spirits, healthy.
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease
wety: Derived from Prakrit 𑀡𑀻𑀭𑁄𑀕 (ṇīroga) from Sanskrit नीरोग (nīroga).

def: adj. & s.m. = nirog, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

def: adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگ निरोग ni-rog [S. नीरोगः, for nis+roga], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
more: adj. S. निरोग Pālī, nirogo . Healthy; sound; wholesome. Nirogī kāyā .
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

def: s.f. = S نيروگتو नीरोगत्व nī-rogatva, s.m. = nīrog, or nirog, q.v.
s.m. = S نيروگتا नीरोगता nī-rogatā, s.f. = nīrog, or nirog, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), = H نروگ निरोग ni-rog [S. नीरोगः, for nis+roga], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
more: adj. In high health and spirits, healthy.
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. ni-rog. adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

def: adj. = nīrog, or nirog, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگت निरोगित ni-rogit [ni-rog(a)+S. इतः], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

ety: nirogī + pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Freedom from sickness, healthiness, health.

adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگ निरोग ni-rog [S. नीरोगः, for nis+roga], adj. (f. -ā), = H نروگا निरोगा ni-rogā [ni-rog(a)+S. कः], adj. (f. -ī), = H نروگي निरोगी ni-rogī [ni-rog(a)+S.इकः (इन्+कः)], adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from sickness, not ill, healthy, in sound health; sound; in good spirits
subs: adv. Well; — s.m. Healthiness; soundness; freedom from sickness or disease

def: s.f. = S نيروگيتو नीरोग्यत्व nī-rogyatva, s.m. = nīrog, or nirog, q.v.
s.m. = S نيروگيتا नीरोग्यता nī-rogyatā, s.f. = nīrog, or nirog, q.v.
adj. = nir-paksh, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Without wings; — without followers or adherents, &c.; friendless, helpless; — not belonging or leaning to any side or party; unprejudiced, impartial.
adj. Helpless, friendless.

adj. Without wings, &c. (i.q. nir-pakshī, q.v.). adj. (f. -inī), Without wings; — without followers or adherents, &c.; friendless, helpless; — not belonging or leaning to any side or party; unprejudiced, impartial.
s.f. The state or condition of having no following or adherents, &c.; want of friends, or of protection; friendlessness; — impartiality.
adj. (f. -inī), Without wings; — without followers or adherents, &c.; friendless, helpless; — not belonging or leaning to any side or party; unprejudiced, impartial.
(prop. nish-phal, q.v.), Bearing no fruit; without fruit, fruitless; unproductive; unprofitable, &c. (i.q. nishphal).
s.m. Dancing; dance; — gesticulation; pantomime
subs: To dance; — to gesticulate (syn. bhāʼo batānā)

def: s.m. (f. -ī), Dancer; actor, mime, mummer, gesticulator; player (of any description); juggler; — a dancing-master.
v.t. (dialec.) To cause to dance, &c. (i.q. naćānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to dance to make (one) dance, to set a-dancing; to spin (a top, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. (causal of نرتنا) To make dance. 2. To make pure, to clean.
subs: v.t. To lead (one) a pretty dance; to tease, worry, harass (here naćā is the past conj. part. of naćānā).

def: v.t. To make pure, to purify, to clean; to clear.
v.n. (dialec.) To dance; to caper skip, jump about playfully; to gesticulate, to play (i.q. nirt karnā; — syn. nāćnā).
more: v. n. To dance, to jump about playfully.

def: s.m. Strength, power.

ety: niruʼār˚ = Prk. निरुवार(इ), fr. S. व्याकृ with निस्
def: v.t. (dialec.) To undo, unravel, disentangle, &c. (syn. kholnā; suljhānā).

part. (f. -ā), Uttered, pronounced, expressed, explained, defined; — unuttered, unspoken, not said, not declared; unexplained; obscure; obsolete; — s.m. Explanation, or etymological interpretation of a word; — name of one of the six Vedāngas or works considered as connected with the Vedas; glossarial explanation of obscure terms (esp. those occurring in the Vedas); name of Yāska's commentary on the Nighaṇṭu (a Vedic glossary).
s.f. Explanation, etymological interpretation of words; — name of the commentary on the Nighaṇṭu by Yāska (i.q. nir-ukta).
adj. (f. -ā), Without form, formless, shapeless; misshapen; — incorporeal; — s.m. A person who is without form; — an epithet of the Supreme Being.
adv. Causelessly, needlessly.
adj. Without guile, guileless; honest; — bold, fearless; — safe, secure.
(dialec.), adv. corr. of nir-upādhi, q.v. adv. Causelessly, needlessly.
corr. of nir-upādhi, q.v. adv. Causelessly, needlessly.

adj. (f. -ā), Secure from hostile attacks, free from ravages; not visited by calamity or affliction, free from adversity or danger; unharmed; peaceful; secure; — s.m. Freedom from hostile attack, &c.; — security, tranquillity, ease.
adj. (f. -ā), Without a compeer or equal, unequalled, peerless, matchless, incomparable, unexampled.
adj. Defining, determining; — s.m. Determining, defining; ascertaining; determination; definition; investigation; explanation; — looking for, searching, seeking; — sight, seeing.
s.m. corr. of نروپن ni-rūpaṇ, q.v.
adj. Without expedient, or remedy; remediless, irretrievable; helpless; — unavailing, unsuccessful.
part. (f. -ā), Appointed, chosen, deputed; directed (to do a thing); — considered, weighed; ascertained, determined, defined; fixed upon, settled, arranged; — explained, set forth.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be seen (or admitting of being seen), or defined, or ascertained.
s.f. = S نروپيتو निरूप्यत्व ni-rūpyatva, s.m. Ascertaining, determining; — ascertainableness; defineableness; — visibility.
s.m. = S نروپيتا निरूप्यता ni-rūpyatā, s.f. Ascertaining, determining; — ascertainableness; defineableness; — visibility.
adj. (f. -ā), Without energy, unenergetic; averse to active exertion; undertaking nothing; unenterprizing; lacking spirit or courage, pusillanimous, cowardly, indolent; indifferent.
s.f. = S نرتساهتو निरुत्साहत्व nir-utsāhatva, s.m. Want of energy; indolence; pusillanimity, cowardice.
s.m. = S نرتساهتا निरुत्साहता nir-utsāhatā, s.f. Want of energy; indolence; pusillanimity, cowardice.
adj. (f. -ā), Answerless, unanswerable; unable to answer, without a reply, silenced, posed, nonplused, without excuse; making no defence (syn. lā-jawāb).
more: adj. Without answer, unable to answer.

def: v.t. (dialec.) = نروارنا niruʼārnā, q.v. v.t. (dialec.) To undo, unravel, disentangle, &c. (syn. kholnā; suljhānā).
s.m. A proverbial expression, a proverb; — interpretation; etymological explanation, etymology; — adj. (f. -ā), Not speaking, speechless, silent; — unobjectionable, unblamable, blameless.
s.m. Obloquy, censure, blame, reproach; — rumour, report; — asseveration, affirmation; decision of a controversy; — absence of dispute or railing.
s.m. Carrying on, conduct, management; accomplishing, &c. (i.q. nibāh, q.v.): — nir-vāh karnā = nibāh karnā.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Conducting, managing; accomplishing, performing, effecting; — one who conducts, &c.; manager; performer, accomplisher, &c.; — (S. nir-vāha-kam), s.m. Connection of cause and effect.
adj. & s.m. See the pop. form nir-bair.
n. f. 1. ( Buddhists ) Dissolution; aunihilation. 2. (Hin.) Emancipation from matter and reunion with the Deity. The nirvān or Budha era reckons from 543 before Christ.

part. adj. (f. -ā), Extinguished (as a fire, &c.); lost, disappeared, extinct; — liberated from existence, dead, deceased; — (S. nir-vāṇam), s.m. Becoming extinguished; disappearance, extinction; dissolution, death; — final emancipation from matter and from the necessity for further transmigration, and reunion with the Supreme Spirit, eternal bliss; — (with Buddhists and Jains) absolute extinction, annihilation.
more: (from निर् prep. and r. वा Blow, go) adj. Extinguished, extinct. s. m. Beatitude, emancipation from matter and reunion with the deity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit निर्वाण (nirvāṇa, “blown or put out, extinguished”).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without family or lineage, &c. (see nir-bans, the pop. form).
s.f. = S نرونشتو निर्वंशत्व nir-vaṅśatva, s.m. The being without family or lineage; childlessness.
s.m. = S نرونشتا निर्वंशता nir-vaṅśatā, s.f. The being without family or lineage; childlessness.
s.m. Pouring out, sprinkling, offering; presentation of funeral offerings to the manes, libation, oblation; — gift, donation, alms.
s.m. Accomplishment, completion, execution.
adj. = nir-bas, q.v. adj. Without power, or authority, or influence, &c.; powerless; helpless; enfeebled.
adj. Free from wind; sheltered from wind; — not windy; calm, still; — s.m. Absence of wind; a calm; — a place sheltered from wind.
s.m. Disgust, loathing (for); — satiety; loathsomeness; despondency; self-disparagement; humility; — grief; complete indifference, disregard of worldly objects; — despair, desperation; — shame.
adj. Not having the Vedas; unscriptural; infidel.
adj. (f. -ā), Unobstructed; uninterrupted; untroubled; secure from impediments; — (S. nir-vighnam), adv. Unobstructedly, freely; securely; — s.m. Absence of obstruction or impediment.
s.f. Unobstructedness; security from impediments; immunity from encounters; safety.
adj. See the pop. form nir-bīj.

adj. Not admitting an alternative; recognizing no such distinction as that of subject and object; — not admitting of difference or otherness (between one's own spirit and the divine essence); — of unchanging purpose; being 'without variableness or shadow of turning' (the Deity; — syn. bhed-rahit).
adj. (f. -ā), Unchanged, unaltered; unchangeable, immutable, uniform.
s.f. Unchangeableness, immutability; uniformity; absence of fickleness.
adj. Powerless, impotent, feeble; without manly courage, unmanly, spiritless, tame.
adj. (f. -ā), Without poison, poisonless; not venomous; deprived of poison.
s.f. See the popular form nir-bisī.
s.f. See the popular form nir-bisī.
adj. Wanting discrimination or judgment, undiscriminating, indiscreet, inconsiderate, thoughtless, foolish.
s.f. = S نروويکتو निर्विवेकत्व nir-vivekatva, s.m. Want of discrimination or judgment, indiscretion; inconsiderateness, &c.
s.m. = S نروويکتا निर्विवेकता nir-vivekatā, s.f. Want of discrimination or judgment, indiscretion; inconsiderateness, &c.
part. (f. -ā), Satisfied, contented, happy, tranquil, at ease; free from occupation or interest; emancipated; — ended, terminated, ceased.

s.f. Complete satisfaction, contentment, tranquillity, bliss, happiness; — final emancipation or liberation from existence; freedom; rest, repose; release; death (in these senses often erroneously written nir-vr̤itti); — completion, accomplishment, conclusion (prop. nir-vr̤itti); — ceasing, abstaining (from), abstention.
part. (f. -ā), Finished, completely done, accomplished.
s.f. Completion, termination, end, conclusion; — result, fruit, reward; — final beatitude (prop. nir-vr̤iti).
more: n. f. Final emancipation; salvation ( mukt ). nir-vritti h., yā pānā , v. n. To obtain final emancipation or salvation.

def: s.m. Exudation of trees or plants, juice, gum, resin, milk, &c.; — extract; decoction; infusion.
s.f. Disunion; (in Gram.) want of connexion or government; — unfitness, inappropriateness, impropriety.
adj. (f. -ā), Disunited, detached; unconnected; unmeaning; illogical; — inappropriate, improper.

r: nis, nir, nih̥, ni, niś, nish
def: prep. or prefix, Out, forth, out of, away from, &c.; — no, not; without; dis-; -less (nis is changed euphonically to nir, nih̥ (in Urdū نِه ni), niś, nish, or ni, according to the letter which follows).
more: n. f. S. निशा Night ( rāt ). nisā-char, nishā-char , n. m. 1. One who prowls about at night. 2. A demon. 3. A thief. nisā-charī , n. f. 1. A female fiend. 2. A harlot. nis din , adv. Night and day; always. Ek triyā hai jal kī bāsī, Nis din jal meṅ rahe piyāsī, Jab muṅh uske paṛe hai būṅd, Ḍubkī khā aur le mukh mūṅd . Riddle. Ans. Sīpī . A pearl shell. A maiden in the water lies, All night and day she thirsty sighs, When in her mouth a drop but goes She 'll take a dive and both lips close.

S. निस् 1. A privative particle. 2. Not; no; without. nis-sat k. , To exhaust; impoverish (the soil). nis-santān , adj. Childless; without issue. nis-sandeh, nis-saṅkh . adj. 1. Undoubted; certain. 2. Sans souci ; free. Āp hile aru mohe hilāve, Vākā hilnā more man bhāve, Hil hilā-ke hoë nisaṅkhā. 'E, sakhī, sājan,?' 'Nā, sakhī, paṅkhā' . Riddle. Mukrī. Himself he shakes and me he shakes, His shaking still my fancy takes, He cares not aught, shakes all he can. 'A lover, dear?' 'No, dear, a fan.' nis-kapaṭ , adj. Open; artless; sincere; honest. nis-kalaṅk , adj. Without stain or flaw; sound.

s. f. Night. نسچر nis-char, s. m. A prowler: see نساچر beneath. نسچراٴي nis-char-āʼī, s. f. Prowling. نسچراٴي کرنا nis-char-āʼī karnā, To prowl. نساچر निशाचर nisā-char, 1. A demon. 2. A robber, a thief. 3. A nocturnal animal, an animal that watches and feeds by night. نساکر يا نساناتهـ निशाचर nisā-kar, or निशानाथ nisā-nāth, The moon.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀡𑀲𑀸 (ṇasā), from Sanskrit स्नसा (snasā).

def: s.f. Night (i.q. niśā)
subs: s.m. See the pop. form nis-ćar; and also niśā-ćar.

r: niś, nish, nis, ćh, k, kh, ṭ, nir
def: = S نش निष् nish Prefix, euphonically substituted for nis, the former before ć, ćh, &c., and the latter before k, kh, ṭ, &c. (see nis; nir).
s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀡𑀲𑀸 (ṇasā), from Sanskrit स्नसा (snasā).

def: (an anomalous pl. fr. the rt. أنس; see ins, and insān), s.f. pl. Women; the female sex.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نسا (nisā), from Arabic نِسَاء (nisāʔ).

def: s.f. Night (see niśā)
more: s. f. Night. نسچر nis-char, s. m. A prowler: see نساچر beneath. نسچراٴي nis-char-āʼī, s. f. Prowling. نسچراٴي کرنا nis-char-āʼī karnā, To prowl. نساچر निशाचर nisā-char, 1. A demon. 2. A robber, a thief. 3. A nocturnal animal, an animal that watches and feeds by night. نساکر يا نساناتهـ निशाचर nisā-kar, or निशानाथ nisā-nāth, The moon.
s. f. plur. 1. Women, ladies. 2. The female sex.
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نسا (nisā), from Arabic نِسَاء (nisāʔ).

def: s.f. Night; — a vision, dream; — turmeric, Curcuma zedoaria, and C. longa
subs: s.m. 'Lord or husband of the night,' the moon
s.m. 'The flower of night,' the white water-lily; — hoar-frost; dew

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or moving about by night, night-walking, nocturnal; — any animal that prowls and feeds by night, nocturnal animal (as a jackal, an owl, &c.); — a Rākshas or demon, goblin, fiend, an evil spirit; — a burglar, a thief
s.f. A she-devil, a female fiend; — a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot; — a sort of perfume
(˚śā+ādī), s.f. The beginning of night, evening, twilight
(˚śā+āk˚), s.m. The form or aspect of night; — adj. Having the appearance of night, night-like
s.m. The time of night, night-time
(˚śā+anta), s.m. End of night, day-break, dawn
(˚śā+an˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Blind at night; — a person who suffers from night-blindness
s.f. (lit. 'blossoming in the night'), The tree Nyctanthes arbor tristis
(˚śā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(˚śā+eta), s.m. (lit. 'shining or white at night') The crane, Ardea nivea.

def: s.f. A certain estate, or number of cattle, for which the poor-rate or a tax is paid; — root, origin, principle; — capital, principal; property; — dignity; — fortune
more: (from نصب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. A root, origin, principle. 2. Dignity, fortune. 3. Capital, principal, property. 4. A certain estate or number of cattle for which a tax is paid. نصاب الصبيان niṣābu-ṣ-ṣibyān (lit. the capital stock of children) The title of an Arabic Vocabulary with Persian translation in rhyme.
subs: 'The capital-stock of children,' title of an Arabic vocabulary with a Persian translation in rhyme.

def: adj. Without noise or sound, noiseless, voiceless, silent, quiet, still; — adv. Without noise, noiselessly.
s.f. = S نشبدتو निःशब्दत्व ni-śabdatva, s.m. Noiselessness, stillness, silence, quiet.
s.m. = S نشبدتا निःशब्दता ni-śabdatā, s.f. Noiselessness, stillness, silence, quiet.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. niśā (see also nis or nisā, and niśi). s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: s.f. = S نسہتو निःसहत्व ni-sahatva, s.m. Inability to bear or support; impatience; unendurance; — insufferableness.
s.m. = S نسہتا निःसहता ni-sahatā, s.f. Inability to bear or support; impatience; unendurance; — insufferableness.
adj. With out helpers, or associates; unassisted.
adj. Of women.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. nisā, q.v.
def: adj. Of or relating to women.
s.m. See s.v. niśā, 'night.' s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: femininity; effeminacy; tenderness, delicacy

ety: Prk. निस्सक्कि or नीसक्की; S. निस्+शक्तिः
def: adj. Powerless, impotent, weak, helpless, poor.
more: adj. ni not, sakat strength. Powerless; poor; weak. Mujh ko kyā nisak samajhā hai?

def: adj. = ni-sak, q.v. adj. Powerless, impotent, weak, helpless, poor.
s.m. A kettledrum (cf. nagārā).
s.m. = نشانه nishāna, q.v. s.m. A mark, sign, an impression; a mark, aim; butt, target; the bull's eye (of a target)
subs: adj. & s.m. Hitting the mark; of unerring aim; — an unerring marksman, a good shot
(-par), To take aim (at), to aim, to point a weapon (at)
or nishāna mārnā, To hit the mark; to strike the bull's eye.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. niśā, 'night.' s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

ety: nisandh = ni-sandhi, q.v.+Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Compactness, solidity, firmness, closeness, tightness.
s. f. Solidity.
adj. corr. of next, q.v.
adj. Solid, tight, not leaky.
adj. Having no joints perceptible; without openings or fissures; well-knit, compact, solid, firm, close, right, not leaky.

adj. Not attached (to), unattached, unconnected, separated, separate; — unimpeded, unobstructed; — not devoted (to) regardless (of), indifferent (to); free from worldly attachment, or selfish desires, unselfish, disinterested.
adj. & adv. See the pop. form nis-saṅk.
adj. & adv. See the pop. form nis-saṅk.
adv. Without difficulty or trouble, easily.
adj. & adv. Without contracting; — without shame or reserve.
more: adj. Without contracting; without shame or reserve.

def: s.m. = nisās, q.v. s.m. Breathing out, expiration; respiration; breath; — sighing, a sigh.
adj. & adv. Undoubted, certain, infallible, unerring; — undoubtedly, doubtless, certainly, &c.
A wrong form for ni-saṉśay, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. niśā, 'night.' s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Tranquillized, tranquil, quiet, patient; — (S. ni-śāntam) s.m. A house, dwelling

ety: Prk. निस्सट्ठो; S. निस्+शस्तः, rt. शंस्
def: adj. Inauspicious, ill-omened, unblest (cf nasīṭh).
adj. Ill-omened.

ety: prob. nisā(na) = nasā(na)+Ap. Prk. एव्वअडअं = S. (इ)तव्य+र+कं
def: s.m. The carrier-pigeon (syn. asāwarī).

s.m. Scattering, throwing, strewing, dispersing, dispersion; — offering, presenting; donative; sacritice; — money which is scattered or thrown among the people at marriages or on other festive occasions (syn. nićhāwar); money presented as an offering to the great (i.q. nićhāwar)
more: n. m. نثر scattering. 1. Money thrown amongst the people at a festival, etc. ( nichhāvar ). 2. A sacrifice. nisār k. , v. a. 1. To scatter money as alms. 2. To sacrifice or devote oneself to.

(from نثر) s. m. f. 1. Scattering, throwing, strewing. 2. Money which is thrown among the people upon festive occasions, donative, sacrifice. نثار کرنا nis̤ār karnā, To scatter, strew, or throw (as a publick donative, &c.), to sacrifice. نثارهونا nis̤ār honā, To be sacrificed.
subs: (-par), To scatter (money, &c. as alms, or a public donative, &c.); to sacrifice, or offer (to or at); to sacrifice or devote (oneself, to or for); to offer (money, &c., to a great personage, as a present)
(-par), To be offered, or sacrificed (to); to be devoted (for).

def: s.m. Going forth or out; coming forth, exit; the egress, or outlet (from a house, &c.); — departure, dying, death; final beatitude; — a means (against), an expedient, a remedy.
adj. Relating to an offering, or sacrifice, &c.

ety: caus. of nisarnā; — nisār˚ = Prk. निस्सारे(इ) or निस्सार(इ) = S. निःसारयति), caus. of rt. निस्+सृ
def: v.t. To cause to go out or away, to turn out, eject, expel; to cause to issue or come forth.
v.n. To come out or forth, to issue; to go out or forth; to depart.
more: v. n. S. नष्कर्षण To issue; go forth; come out.
v. n. To issue, to go forth, to come out.

def: v.n. = nisarnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to go out or away, to turn out, eject, expel; to cause to issue or come forth.
more: v. n. To issue, to go forth, to come out.

def: v.n. = nisarnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to go out or away, to turn out, eject, expel; to cause to issue or come forth.
more: (in Braj) v. n. See نيسرنا nīsarnā.

ety: Prk. निस्सासो and निस्सासओ; S. निःश्वासः and निः श्वास+कः
def: s.m. Breathing out, expiration; respiration; breath; — sighing, a sigh.

ety: Prk. निस्सासो and निस्सासओ; S. निःश्वासः and निः श्वास+कः
def: s.m. Breathing out, expiration; respiration; breath; — sighing, a sigh.
part. (f. -ā), Sharpened, whetted; sharp; — polished, burnished; — varnished.
adj. Untrue, false, insincere.
s.f. Untrueness, falseness, insincerity, lying disposition; — falsehood, untruth.

ety: for A. نسبة, inf. n. of نسب; see nasab
def: s.f. Referring (to, -se); deriving (from); — reference, respect, regard (to); attribute; relation, connexion; affinity; analogy; comparison; — ratio; proportion; — relationship by marriage; matrimonial alliance; betrothal; — a relation, or connexion; — a conundrum; — adv. or postpn. With reference (to, -kī), in respect (of), respecting; — in comparison (with); in proportion (to)
more: n. f. 1. Relation (تعلق 1, 2). 2. Affinity; analogy. 3. Ratio; proportion. 4. Betrothal; relationship by marriage. 5. A relation; connection. 6. A conundrum. nisbat rakhnā , v. n. To bear relation to ( taalluq rakhnā ). nisbat k. , v. a. 1. To ascribe; attribute; impute. 2. To refer or assign to. 3. To betroth; form an alliance with. nisbat-nāmā, kursī-nāmah, nasab-nāmah , n. m. A genealogical table. nisbat ho jānā , v. n. To be betrothed. nisbat , adv. 1. With respect, relation, or reference to; respecting. 2. In comparison with; than; in proportion to.
s. f. 1. Relation, affinity, respecting, regarding, attribute, reference. 2. (dakh.) The ceremony of betrothing: see مانگني māngnī. نسبت کرنا nisbat karnā, To refer, to attribute.
subs: (-se), To have reference (to); to bear relation (to); to have to do (with)
v.t. To refer (a thing, to, -se), to derive (from); to ascribe, attribute, or impute (to); to accuse, or charge (a person, with, -se); — to form a matrimonial alliance with; to betroth
To be or become connected by marriage; to become betrothed
The affix ي, denoting kindred and relationship.

def: comparatively, relatively
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نِسْبَتاً (nisbatan), from Arabic نِسْبَةً (nisbatan).

def: adj. Related (to), having reference (to); related by marriage, connected; — s.m. Wife's brother, brother-in-law (i.q. nisbatī bhāʼī).
more: adj. 1. Related or having reference to. 2. Related by marriage. nis'batī, nisbatī bhāī , Mah. n. m. A wife's brother; brother-in-law.
1. adj. Related, having reference to. 2. s. m. A wifeʼs brother.

def: s.m. corr. of نشچی niś-ćay, q.v. s.m. Ascertainment, investigation, inquiry, determination, adjudication, decision; — accurate or certain knowledge; fixed opinion; positive conclusion; assurance, certainty; — trust, belief, faith; — settled purpose, fixed intention, resolve; design, aim; — adj. Ascertained, indubitable, certain, sure; actual, real; — adv. For a certainty, certainly, assuredly, indubitably, positively, really, actually
subs: or niśćai rahnā (-ko), To have (the) assurance (of a thing, -kā), to feel certain
v.n. To be ascertained, to become clear (to), &c.
(-kā), To ascertain; to determine; to decide, to pass judgment; to make sure (of), to convince oneself (of), to verify, to prove; to conclude; to determine, resolve; — to ratify; to guarantee

def: s.m. &c. = niś-ćay, q.v. (and cf. nih-ćai). s.m. Ascertainment, investigation, inquiry, determination, adjudication, decision; — accurate or certain knowledge; fixed opinion; positive conclusion; assurance, certainty; — trust, belief, faith; — settled purpose, fixed intention, resolve; design, aim; — adj. Ascertained, indubitable, certain, sure; actual, real; — adv. For a certainty, certainly, assuredly, indubitably, positively, really, actually
more: 1. s. m. Certainty, ascertainment; trust, belief, faith. 2. adj. Actual, real, ascertained, certain, sure, indubitable. 3. adv. Certainly, actually, indubitably.
subs: or niśćai rahnā (-ko), To have (the) assurance (of a thing, -kā), to feel certain
v.n. To be ascertained, to become clear (to), &c.
(-kā), To ascertain; to determine; to decide, to pass judgment; to make sure (of), to convince oneself (of), to verify, to prove; to conclude; to determine, resolve; — to ratify; to guarantee

def: adj. corr. of نشچل niś-ćal, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Not moving, still; immovable, fixed, steady; — not subject to motion or fluctuation; invariable, unchangeable
subs: (˚la+an˚), s.m. A species of crane, Ardea nivea; — a rock, mountain.

def: s.f. The earth.

adj. & s.m. See s.v. nis, 'night' (and see niśā-ćar). prep. or prefix, Out, forth, out of, away from, &c.; — no, not; without; dis-; -less (nis is changed euphonically to nir, nih̥ (in Urdū نِه ni), niś, nish, or ni, according to the letter which follows).
s.f. See the pop. form nis-ćarāʼī, s.v. nis or nisā.

r: niś-ćay, niś-ćaʼe, niś-ćai, nis-ćai, niśćai, nisćai, ći, nis
def: s.m. Ascertainment, investigation, inquiry, determination, adjudication, decision; — accurate or certain knowledge; fixed opinion; positive conclusion; assurance, certainty; — trust, belief, faith; — settled purpose, fixed intention, resolve; design, aim; — adj. Ascertained, indubitable, certain, sure; actual, real; — adv. For a certainty, certainly, assuredly, indubitably, positively, really, actually
more: 1. s. m. Certainty, ascertainment, trust, belief, faith. 2. adj. Actual, real, ascertained. adv. Actually, certainly, indubitably. See نسچي nischai.
subs: or niśćai rahnā (-ko), To have (the) assurance (of a thing, -kā), to feel certain
v.n. To be ascertained, to become clear (to), &c.
(-kā), To ascertain; to determine; to decide, to pass judgment; to make sure (of), to convince oneself (of), to verify, to prove; to conclude; to determine, resolve; — to ratify; to guarantee

def: adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of motion; incapable of motion or effort; motionless; powerless, helpless.
adj. (f. -ā), Having no rent or openings or gaps, without holes; without weak points, or flaws, or defects; having no weak side; — uninterrupted; unhurt.
adj. corr. of niś-ćhidra, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Having no rent or openings or gaps, without holes; without weak points, or flaws, or defects; having no weak side; — uninterrupted; unhurt.
adj. (f. -ā), See the pop. form nićint.
adj. corr. of نشچنت niś-ćint, q.v. (and cf. ni-ćint).
s.f. = nićintāʼī, q.v. s.f. Freedom from care or anxiety, &c.; leisure; thoughtlessness, heedlessness, unconcern, fearlessness.
s.f. = niśćintāʼī.
part. (f. -ā), Ascertained, determined, decided, established, certain, settled, concluded; — (S. niśćitam) s.m. Certainty; decision, conclusion; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, decidedly, positively
adj. corr. of نشچت niś-ćit, q.v.
s.m. A sort of tooth-powder (prepared from sulphate of iron, &c., which destroys the tartar, but blackens the teeth).
